transform_flags: case_id SEQ provisional (from CDV-047) - maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-CARD-01 bucket

difficulty_tier straightforward (01), diagnosis_category common - a classic textbook emotionally/blood-injury triggered reflex (vasovagal) faint where every feature points benign and all dangerous-syncope discriminators are negative; modelled as an over-triage-avoidance/reassurance case, with the benign-vs-dangerous discrimination reasoning carried in 10/11

sex_at_birth not stated in source and not reliably inferable, but schema requires it - placeholder value 'female' assigned arbitrarily (no clinical signal either way, not diagnostically relevant to this benign faint); MUST be confirmed/corrected by clinician on review
age 24 taken directly from source (adult); next of kin recorded as spouse (present and available)

telehealth reprojection applied - the source is an in-person urgent home assessment (neurological examination, cardiac auscultation, active-stand orthostatic vitals, palpation for head trauma) reprojected to telehealth_chat; the trigger, prodrome, witnessed pallor/limp fall, rapid recovery and patient self-report of no injury are patient/bystander-reportable and kept in 01/02, while clinician examination findings, negative orthostatic vitals and the baseline ECG are sealed in 10/11 + telehealth_limits

presentation layer de-anchored - the source SOAP note was written by a clinician who already knew the diagnosis; 01/02 rewritten as the patient/spouse would present before the diagnosis was known, with no diagnosis name, tier or 'these are the red flags' labelling

bystander witnessed-event account and patient self-report of no injury retained in 01.objective_data_offered as caregiver_reported/patient_reported and unverified, with reliability caveats; clinician-measured examination/orthostatic values kept sealed

no medications indicated for this benign faint - 12.medications left as an empty array (no dose invented); management is reassurance, counter-pressure manoeuvres, hydration, trigger avoidance and a routine baseline ECG

baseline tier T1 (routine GP within 1-2 weeks) - LOW/conservative-management tier reflecting a fully recovered, red-flag-negative classic reflex faint with no injury from the fall (differs from the head-strike neighbour CDV-043 which was T3 and the fall-risk elderly neighbour CDV-031 which was T2); robust safety-netting retained with ESC to T5 for delayed head-injury signs (ESC-001) and for a dangerous exertional/unheralded/cardiac recurrence (ESC-002)

the source's tilt-table finding was 'clinically assumed due to classic history' (not performed) - encoded as a NOT-recommended investigation for a classic uncomplicated first episode, not as an obtained result
no allergies, family history, or guideline citations in source - left as empty arrays/omitted, not fabricated
empty arrays used where the source is silent (comorbidities, risk_scores, allied_health_referrals, medications, guidelines_referenced, PMH, allergies, family history)

candidate SNOMED/ICD-10-AM codes assigned from knowledge and registered unverified (ICD-10-AM R55 'Syncope and collapse' used for the primary diagnosis as vasovagal syncope has no distinct ICD-10-AM code); all pending terminology-server verification

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
